name: Geometric Shapes Carousel
aesthetic: Bold geometric pop — flat pink / cobalt blue / school-bus yellow color blocks, oversized oval/circle/heart/scalloped-circle shape cutouts that frame photos or sit as solid color fields. Display serif italic + black sans headlines, high-contrast three-tone palette. Feels like a modern editorial social pack: flat, confident, no gradients.

fonts.display_italic: Fraunces / Playfair Display (italic display, used for accent word)

slides.subject: Pink background; large blue vertical oval centered-left frames the hero dish photo; italic display headline top-right with bold accent word beside it; meta URL top, handle bottom.
Blue background; big yellow scalloped-circle (flower-petal edge) frames the feature dish; italic 'New' + bold 'Product' stack top-left; big date/time stamp right.
Yellow background; oversized pink heart shape center-right, italic + bold headline stack top-left, pill date under headline, handle bottom-left.
Pink background; 3 pink circles (two small top, one large bottom) each masking a food photo; bold italic two-line headline top-left; handle footer.
Yellow top half + blue bottom half split with pink oval wrapping the BiteMap logo in center; 'catch you on' script-italic above logo; app store badges bottom.
slides.copy: Fixed: 'found on' / BiteMap logo / tagline / app badges

copy_rules: Each content slide pairs ONE italic word/phrase with ONE bold word/phrase — never two italics or two bolds
Keep headlines short: italic 1-2 words, bold 1-2 words. Long copy breaks the shape-block rhythm.
Never use generic hype ('amazing', 'must-try'). Name the literal thing.
Restaurant name lives in the @handle / kicker only — never in the big display words
asset_rules: Photos must crop cleanly inside oval / circle / heart masks — pick images where the subject is centered
Minimum 1080px wide; real photos only (no AI)
High-saturation photos play best against the flat color blocks — avoid washed-out interiors
use_when: Editorial / modern restaurants where flat color-block design reads premium
Menu launches / product drops / event announcements where a date or 'new' beat is the hook
Cuisines that photograph colorfully (pasta, dessert, brunch plates, cocktails)
Campaigns that need to feel curated and magazine-like rather than hand-drawn or street
